_meta.description: REAL capture, converted to DetectionEvent shapes. Do not hand-edit the events without updating this file's source pointers below.
_meta.video: kZwWv_2SDgU — Elisa True Crime, ~138 min (spike/RESULTS.md §2 row 4, 'yellow-class (low, off-dataset)')
_meta.note: This is the hypothesized LIMITED signature (spike/RESULTS.md §3 finding 4, §4): uncalibrated, single low-confidence channel, direction not proof. classify() and this test assert only the observed DETAIL (preroll/midrolls/postroll/density) — the client NEVER infers a 'yellow' verdict (SPEC §1.1, §3.3): that inference happens server-side, with calibrated thresholds, not here.

sourceRecords.note: RESULTS.md: 'one ad at t=0 (from adSlots)'. Source A's placements have NO preroll kind, but the DOM saw an ad start at content time 0. classify() gives source A precedence when placements are present (SPEC §3.2 table: 'present' rows use A's own preroll/midroll/postroll split; B only overrides when A is absent, row 4) — so this fixture's expected preroll stays FALSE, straight from the placement data, despite this DOM sighting. Chronologically FIRST (21:02:57.920Z), ~4.7s before the confirmation read below — the `events` array below is ordered by real capturedAt, not by source.
Only placement-bearing response captured: 0 preroll, 5 midroll, 1 postroll placements (count 6, no AD_PLACEMENT_KIND_START) — RESULTS.md's '0/5/1'.
